overview.short: Taranga is a Jain pilgrimage center near Kheralu in Mehsana district, Gujarat, India, with two compounds of Jain temples that are important examples of the Māru-Gurjara style of ar
overview.description: Taranga is a Jain pilgrimage center near Kheralu in Mehsana district, Gujarat, India, with two compounds of Jain temples that are important examples of the Māru-Gurjara style of architecture. The Ajitnatha temple, was constructed in 1161 by the Solanki king Kumarapala, under the advice of his teacher, Acharya Hemachandra. Both the main sects of Jainism are represented, with adjoining walled compounds: the Śvetāmbara compound consists of fourteen temples in all, and there are also five Digambara-affiliated temples at Taranga hill.

topPlaces.name: Nava Sudasana
topPlaces.description: Nava sudasana is a village in Satlasana Taluka, Mehsana district in the Indian state of Gujarat, India. The population of village is around 6,500.

items.note: Nava sudasana is a village in Satlasana Taluka, Mehsana district in the Indian state of Gujarat, India. The population of village is around 6,500.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Taranga?
faq.a: The best time to visit Taranga is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (100 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (106 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Taranga?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Taranga at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Taranga?
faq.a: Stays in Taranga start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Taranga?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nava Sudasana.
